items.scenario: You regularly use a small public library branch in your neighborhood. The branch recently shortened its weekend hours to cut costs, and you have noticed that far fewer students are using its quiet study space as a result.
items.direction: Write an email to the branch librarian. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how you usually use the library on weekends
Explain how the reduced hours affect local students
Suggest an alternative arrangement the branch could try
items.subject: Weekend hours at the neighborhood branch

items.scenario: You recently signed up for an annual subscription to an online language-learning app. After the first month, several lessons you had already paid for vanished from your account, and the support page has failed to load for days.
items.direction: Write an email to the company's support team contact. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the missing lessons
Explain when the lessons first disappeared
Request a way to restore your full access
items.subject: Missing lessons on my annual plan

items.scenario: You live in a campus apartment and share a laundry room with several other units. One of the two washing machines has been out of order for over two weeks, causing long waits during the busy evening hours.
items.direction: Write an email to the housing manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the current situation in the laundry room
Explain how the broken machine affects residents
Ask when a repair can be scheduled
items.subject: Out-of-order washing machine in the laundry room

items.scenario: You recently finished a three-month internship at a design studio, where a senior colleague mentored you closely. You hope to stay involved with the team on a part-time basis once your classes resume in the fall.
items.direction: Write an email to your former mentor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your mentor for the guidance you received
Explain your interest in continuing part-time
Ask about possible openings for the coming term
items.subject: Staying involved with the studio part-time
